description_long: This is a dramatic, fantasy-inspired double-headed axe featuring a dark metallic blade shaped like two outstretched bat wings, each side mirroring the other. The blade is mounted atop a cylindrical handle that appears to be made of wood or a wood-like material, reinforced with ornate metallic fittings at both ends and the center. The top of the axe is adorned with two dragon or serpent heads facing outward, adding a mythical and intimidating flair. The handle is straight, with subtle texturing and banded segments for improved grip and visual interest. The overall color palette is dominated by black and dark brown, with metallic highlights and a slightly glossy finish. This axe is more decorative or ceremonial than utilitarian, likely intended as a display piece or for theatrical use in an urban fantasy context.
description: A double-headed axe with bat wing-shaped black blades and a straight, banded handle, adorned with dragon head details. The design is ornate and fantasy-themed, with dark metallic and wood-like materials.
description_view_0: Front view showing both bat wing-shaped blades and the twin dragon heads at the top, with the full handle visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blades and the cylindrical handle, with minimal blade width visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the symmetrical bat wing blades and dragon heads from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin blade profile and the straight handle.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical handle, likely wood with metallic bands, subtle texturing for grip, no curvature.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed blade shaped like bat wings, two cutting edges, decorative dragon heads at the top, no beard or poll.
attribute_mounting_style: Decorative, likely modern composite mounting with integrated blade and handle fittings.
